label: remove repulsion shell, zero damping for true conservative dynamics
notes: Reverted move_008's hard repulsion shell — the bouncing-on-contact behavior wasn't what was wanted. The actual collapse cause was velocity damping draining orbital energy over time. Set damping=0 (was 0.02) so the gravity sim is now energetically conservative — total energy preserved indefinitely → motion preserved indefinitely → no collapse. Bodies can now pass through each other on close approaches (softening eps2 still caps gravity force at finite max so passes are smooth, not explosive). The min_dist and repulsion_k channels remain in ctrl_motes but are unused by the physics now (the args are still passed through for code stability — easy to revert if Nick wants the shell back later for a different look). Central restoring + escape pull-back still active to keep the cloud bounded inside the orb.

operations.before_summary: Lennard-Jones (min_dist/r)^4 - 1 close-range repulsion; bodies bounced apart when r<min_dist
operations.after_summary: Repulsion block removed; bodies can pass through each other freely; softened gravity caps acceleration at max
